findings.description: ownerAddress is interpolated into the auth widget script via template literal: var ownerAddress = '${chatSettings.ownerAddress}'. If ownerAddress contained a single quote, it could break the script. However, ownerAddress is validated elsewhere (regex ^0x[0-9a-fA-F]{40}$) so it can never contain a quote.
findings.reason: ownerAddress is validated at write time (bot.ts) with Ethereum address regex. No injection risk.

findings.description: Nonce is generated with crypto.randomBytes(16).toString('hex') producing 32 hex characters. This is 128 bits of entropy, sufficient for CSRF protection.

Nonce is stored in session only if webchat_session cookie exists and matches a valid session. If no session exists, nonce is still embedded in HTML but not stored server-side, meaning the OAuth callback will fail nonce validation. This is correct per spec: anonymous visitors without sessions cannot complete OAuth flow with nonce protection.
findings.reason: By design: session-less visitors get a new nonce on next page load after session creation.

findings.description: CSP connect-src is extended only for protected dashboards. Unprotected dashboards retain strict 'self' only. This minimizes attack surface.

summary: No security vulnerabilities found. Nonce generation uses crypto.randomBytes (CSPRNG), ownerAddress is pre-validated, CSP is conditionally extended only when needed. The GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID is a public-safe value (standard for OAuth client_id in browser apps). The state parameter in the OAuth redirect is properly base64-encoded with nonce for CSRF protection.
